title: Oven cleaner paste
subtitle: A bicarbonate and washing soda paste for baked-on oven grease
excerpt: A thick paste of bicarbonate of soda, washing soda, and liquid castile soap that loosens baked-on oven grease when left overnight. Spread inside the oven, leave for 8 to 12 hours, then wipe and rinse. No harsh fumes.
sourceNotes: Alkaline mechanism of grease removal: sodium bicarbonate (pH 8.3) and sodium carbonate (washing soda, pH 11.4) saponify fats on contact, loosening baked-on grease. This is the same chemistry as cold-process soap making at a more dilute and consumer-safe scale. Standard household cleaning reference: Linda Mason Hunter and Mikki Halpin, The Healthy Home Workbook (2007).

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 batch (~300 g, sufficient for one standard oven interior)
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make fresh for each use. Apply in the evening and wipe out in the morning.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Make fresh and use within a week. If stored, the paste dries out and loses effectiveness. Do not seal in an airtight container as CO2 released by the bicarbonate can pressurise the container.

glossaryTerms.definition: In cleaning, sodium bicarbonate and washing soda saponify the fatty acids in baked-on grease, breaking the bond between the grease and the oven surface. The same principle as soap making, but at a milder, consumer-safe concentration.

content.text: Commercial oven cleaners use strong caustic alkalis that work fast but require gloves, ventilation, and careful rinsing. This paste works on the same principle at a much lower concentration. Bicarbonate of soda is mildly alkaline; washing soda is more strongly alkaline. Together they begin the

of baked fats over several hours, loosening the grease from the enamel. The liquid castile soap helps lift the loosened grease during wipe-down. The method needs an overnight dwell; the result is no fumes and no chemical burns. It works on moderate oven soiling. Very heavy or carbonised deposits may need a second application.

items.prepNote: Main alkaline base and mild abrasive
Sodium carbonate; increases alkalinity for heavy grease
Surfactant to help lift loosened grease during wipe-down
Add gradually to reach a thick, spreadable paste

content.text: Combine the bicarbonate of soda and washing soda in a bowl and stir to combine. Add the liquid castile soap and stir; the mixture will fizz as the acid in the soap reacts briefly with the bicarbonate. Add water a tablespoon at a time until the paste reaches a consistency that spreads without dripping. Wear gloves when handling; washing soda is irritating to skin in direct contact.

Make sure the oven is cold before starting. Remove the oven racks and clean them separately. Spread the paste over the oven interior with a silicone spatula, avoiding the heating elements. The paste will be pale grey and thick. Leave for 8 to 12 hours, or overnight.

After the dwell time, the paste will have dried and absorbed into the grease deposits. Wipe out the interior with a damp cloth, working in sections. Rinse the cloth frequently. A final wipe with a cloth wrung out in a 50/50 white vinegar and water solution neutralises any remaining bicarbonate residue and leaves the oven interior clean.

Safe for standard enamel oven interiors and glass doors. Do not apply to the heating elements, fan openings, or any rubber seals. Do not use on self-cleaning oven surfaces (the coating can be damaged).
Make fresh for each use. The paste dries out and loses effectiveness within a week. Do not seal in an airtight container.
